summary: react-jsonwebtoken typosquats jsonwebtoken and fetches+executes remote code on require
details: The package name impersonates the widely-used `jsonwebtoken` library, and `package.json` metadata (author `auth0`, repository pointing to `radix-ui/primitives`) is fabricated to reinforce the impersonation. The main module `decode.js` runs a top-level async function on `require()` that base64-decodes a hidden host (`jsonkeeper.com`) and a hidden path, `axios.get`s a JSON document from that endpoint, and passes the response's `errCode` field to `new Function.constructor('require', errCode)`, then invokes the constructed function with the real `require`. This yields arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution in the installing project's Node process on any import of the package, with full access to `require` (and therefore filesystem, network, child_process, and installed dependencies). The destination is under attacker control and mutable, so the payload can change at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
